# Borough — Copilot Instructions

Borough is a deterministic, headless life-simulation game: a medieval town is generated, NPCs live/die/marry/work, and a player can be born into that same persistent world. It lives in the `/home/command/borough` directory of the larger `oca` repository.

## Build, test, and run

- No build step; pure Python 3.10+.
- The package relies on runtime `PYTHONPATH=.` because tests and `play.py` import `core.*` and `engine.*` as top-level modules.
- Install optional dev dependency from the workspace root:
  ```bash
  pip install -e "..[dev]"   # pytest only declared dependency
  pip install Pillow         # required for engine/render.py and Sprint 6 tests
  ```
- Run the full test suite:
  ```bash
  cd /home/command/borough
  PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/
  ```
- Run a single sprint file:
  ```bash
  PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_sprint0.py -q
  ```
- Run one test:
  ```bash
  PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_sprint0.py::test_save_load_roundtrip -q
  ```
- Tests can also be executed directly (they print acceptance-style output):
  ```bash
  cd /home/command/borough
  PYTHONPATH=. python tests/test_sprint0.py
  ```
- Play the game (interactive Pygame tileset GUI):
  ```bash
  cd /home/command/borough
  PYTHONPATH=. python gui.py
  ```
- Play the game (interactive ncurses TUI):
  ```bash
  cd /home/command/borough
  PYTHONPATH=. python tui.py
  ```
- Play the game (headless / console scenes):
  ```bash
  cd /home/command/borough
  PYTHONPATH=. python play.py --seed 1729 --years 60 --lives 1
  ```
  Use `--fast` to skip per-year waits, `--render-dir ./maps` to write map PNGs.

## High-level architecture

### Entry points
- `play.py` is the human-facing loop. It generates or resumes a `World`, spawns a `player` via `core.player.spawn_player`, steps the `Simulation`, renders scenes to the console, and can export map images via `engine.render.render_map`.
- `tests/test_sprint*.py` are acceptance tests covering generations sprints 0 and 2–8.

### World model (`core/world.py`)
- `World` is the authoritative container: seed, year/day, map tiles, `npcs`, `buildings`, `contracts`, `items`, and `chronicle`.
- `generate_world(seed, name, year, pop)` creates a deterministic town.
- Seeds may be `str | int | None`; `_coerce_seed` normalizes to a string and `seed_to_int` hashes it into a 64-bit int for `random.Random`.
- Subsystems are optional/lazily attached: `family`, `ghost` (sprint 7), and `reputation`, `weather_state`, `animals`, `factions`, `crises`, `religion`, `dialogue_log` (sprint 8).

### Simulation loop (`core/simulation.py`)
- `Simulation(world, seed=None)` owns a private `random.Random` instance derived from the world seed.
- `DAYS_PER_YEAR = 360`; `day_in_year` wraps `0..359`.
- `tick_day()` advances weather, crises, religion, processes every living NPC (eat/sleep/work/health), propagates gossip, and triggers yearly maintenance.
- `_on_year_boundary()` is the heavy annual phase: aging, memory decay, item/contract caps, deaths, births, marriages, apprenticeships, skill teaching, market/loans, animal ticks, faction cooling, reputation decay, and crisis triggers.
- `run_days(n)` / `run_years(n)` are the public drivers.

### NPCs (`core/npc.py`)
- `NPC` is a dataclass with `id`, names, family, `body`, `mind` (`TraitSet`), `memory`, `knowledge`, `relationships`, and `status`.
- IDs are generated by a module-level counter `_npc_counter` (`n_1`, `n_2`, …). When loading a saved world, IDs are taken as-is, so counters can drift; new code should not rely on counter state after a load.
- `age` is computed externally from `world.year - birth_year`; the `age` property currently returns `0`.
- `LifecycleState` bands: infant (0–3), child (4–12), adolescent (13–17), adult (18–59), elder (60+).

### Contracts (`core/contract.py`)
- Contracts encode marriages, debts, apprenticeships, and inheritance claims.
- They outlive NPCs: debts and apprenticeships can be inherited/disputed when a party dies.
- Status values: `ACTIVE`, `FULFILLED`, `EXPIRED`, `BROKEN`, `DISPUTED`.

### Chronicle and salience (`core/chronicle.py`)
- `Chronicle(world)` writes notable events into `world.chronicle`.
- Town events are recorded automatically; player events are gated by `record_player_event` with a `salience >= 0.3` threshold.
- The same feed is mirrored to `world.ghost` if a ghost ledger exists.

### Player lifecycle (`core/player.py`)
- `spawn_player(world)` marks an existing baby born this year as the player, or force-spawns an orphan/newborn if none exists.
- `on_player_death(world, chronicle)` computes whether the town remembers the player (>= 5 NPCs with high affinity/trust) and may leave a ghost chronicle entry.

### Family and ghosts (`core/family.py`, `core/ghost.py`)
- `FamilyRegistry` tracks parent links, tombstones, and feud state. It gates marriage via line-of-descent incest checks.
- `GhostCausalLedger` stores ghosts of remembered dead and receives chronicle feeds; ghosts have a `threshold` of remembrance.

### Subsystems added in sprint 8
- `reputation.py`: town and per-NPC opinion scores with tiers and decay.
- `weather.py`: deterministic season/weather rolls that affect `work_modifier`.
- `animal.py`: livestock registry with yearly food/death/birth ticks.
- `faction.py`: guilds/militias with member/tension state.
- `crisis.py`: probabilistic plagues/famines/fires with affected NPCs and deaths.
- `religion.py`: temple calendar, tithes, faith, rituals.
- `dialogue.py`: procedural greetings/lines tied to reputation and occupation.

### Rendering (`engine/render.py`)
- Uses the Urizen Onebit v2.0 tileset located at `/home/command/Downloads/urizen_onebit_tileset__v2d0.png`.
- Functions: `render_map(world, path)`, `render_portrait(npc, path)`, `render_scene_visual(scene, path, world)`.
- PNG outputs are validated in `tests/test_sprint6.py`.

### Persistence (`core/persistence.py`)
- JSON save/load, one file per town.
- `save_world(world, path)` / `load_world(path)`.
- `World.from_dict` restores all subsystems and tolerates missing fields from older saves.

## Key conventions

### Determinism
- All simulation RNG should come from `self.rng` (or an explicitly seeded `random.Random`). Do not use the global `random` module for gameplay-affecting logic.
- World generation, weather rolls, and crises are deterministic given a seed.

### Module import style
- Internal imports use `from .module import X` inside `core/` and `from core.module import X` in tests/play.
- Because `borough` is not installed as a package, tests insert `..` into `sys.path` and import `core.*` directly. Use the same style in new tests.

### Mutation and containment
- Add new entities through the world: `world.add_npc(npc)`, `world.add_contract(c)`, `world.add_item(item)`, `world.add_building(b)`.
- Avoid mutating `world.npcs` directly unless you also maintain family/ghost registries.
- Items live in `world.items` and are referenced from `building.item_ids` or `npc.status.inventory_item_ids`.

### Performance/memory caps
- The simulation caps items at ~2000 (trimming oldest non-sacred items) and contracts at ~1000 (dropping fulfilled/broken/expired oldest first).
- Houses cap stored items at ~80 and total items at ~60.
- Recompute `building.food_count_cache` on year boundaries; rely on it for hunger lookups.

### Scenes (`core/scene.py`)
- Scenes are procedural yearly events for the player: birth, apprenticeship offers, marriage proposals, crimes, deaths, etc.
- `pick_scene_for_year(world, player, chronicle)` selects a scene; `render_scene_for_console(scene)` formats it.
- `scene.apply_choice(index, world, chronicle)` mutates world state and should record notable outcomes to the chronicle.

### Save compatibility
- `NPC.from_dict`, `World.from_dict`, and subsystem `from_dict` methods tolerate missing legacy keys. Preserve that tolerance when adding new fields.

### Testing
- Sprint tests are integration/acceptance tests, not unit-isolated. They exercise full world generation and multi-year simulation.
- Rendering tests require the external tileset PNG; if absent, Sprint 6 tests will fail.
